Use this skill when orienting in an unfamiliar codebase before making changes.

## Concepts (timeless)
- Map before you touch: entry points → main modules → data stores → external deps.
- Read the build/run/test commands and the CI config to learn how the project actually ships.
- Trace one real request/flow end-to-end to understand the layering before editing.
- Find the conventions (error handling, config, logging, tests) and follow them — consistency over personal preference.
- Identify the protected/load-bearing areas (migrations, auth, money paths) and tread carefully there.

## Pull current docs first
Read the project's OWN docs first; then confirm any framework specifics:
- the project's README/CONTRIBUTING/ADRs and docs/CODEMAPS/ (run /codemap)
- README, CONTRIBUTING, ADRs, and `docs/CODEMAPS/` if present (run `/codemap` to generate it).

## Checklist
- [ ] Entry points, main modules, and data stores identified
- [ ] Build/test/run commands confirmed (from docs + CI)
- [ ] One core flow traced end-to-end
- [ ] Project conventions noted and followed
- [ ] Load-bearing/protected areas flagged before editing
